why: The 64x7168x2048 shape remains one of the biggest contributors. This isolates lower warp count on the current winning 16x128x256 fused path to test whether reduced pressure helps without changing tile geometry or pipelining.

what: Split-K path uses BLOCK_SIZE_K=512, NUM_KSPLIT=2, BLOCK_SIZE_N=64, num_stages=2, and GROUP_SIZE_M=2
why: On the 16x2112x7168 shape, the two-way split cuts reduction cost sharply but needs more grid exposure than the original BSN128 branch. BSN64 restores occupancy and GROUP_SIZE_M=2 should keep the two M tiles traversing the same B tiles back-to-back.
override: This replays the strongest archived BSN64 split-K branch in the current session for fresh benchmark data.

what: Resubmit the known best baseline unchanged as a control candidate
why: Both the new candidate branches and recent baseline controls are being reported as test FAIL immediately, while benchmark also finishes with unparseable output. A fresh baseline control is the fastest way to determine whether the problem is in the runner/parser rather than the kernel.
override: Current session needs a fresh control after parser-looking failures on both experimental branches.

what: Resubmit the known best baseline unchanged as a control candidate
